name: Chocolate Nut Shortbread
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Unsalted butter, melted and cooled slightly
1/2 cup Finely chopped walnuts or pecans
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300 degrees F (150 degrees C).
2. Cover a baking sheet with parchment paper. Draw two circles, each measuring 7 to 7-1/2 inches in diameter.
3. In a food processor or large bowl, combine flour, cocoa powder, sugar, and salt. Process or mix until combined.
4. Add the melted butter and finely chopped chocolate. Mix until the mixture resembles moist sand.
5. Stir in the walnuts or pecans and additional sugar. The dough should be firm but pliable.
6. Shape the dough into a smooth ball. Cut the ball into four equal pieces, then each piece into two more, resulting in eight quarters.
7. Place each quarter onto one of the circles on the parchment paper. Gently flatten each round to about 1/2 inch from the edge of the circle.
8. Use a fork to make indentations or impressions on the top surface of each round. Round off the edges with the fork as well.
9. Sprinkle each round with a teaspoon of sugar.
10. Bake for 60 minutes, or until the shortbread is lightly golden brown.
11. Allow the shortbread to cool completely before cutting into wedges using a straight-edged knife. Yield: approximately 32 wedges.
